name: Pattern Break
description: Tests whether the system detects and remembers when routines change. Recent changes should surface above the old patterns.

messages.content: My daily routine is pretty set. Standup at 9am, then I dig into whatever's on the sprint board. We use Kafka for all our event streaming — been running it for about two years now.
Sounds like a well-established workflow. Kafka's been reliable for that long?
Rock solid. I usually grab coffee at Peet's on the way in — their dark roast is my go-to. Then standup, then heads-down coding until lunch.
Nice routine. Having that consistent start to the day helps with focus.
Same old, same old. 9am standup, Kafka pipeline maintenance, coffee at Peet's. Though today I had to debug a consumer lag issue that took most of the afternoon.
Consumer lag can be tricky — was it a partition rebalancing issue or something with the consumer group?
Partition skew. One topic had way more traffic than the others. Fixed it by repartitioning. Anyway, back to the grind tomorrow.
Classic Kafka issue. At least it's a straightforward fix once you identify the skew.
Big changes at work! We moved standup to 2pm because half the team is now in Europe. It's weird having my mornings free but honestly I'm way more productive.
That's a significant shift — mornings for deep work instead of starting with standup. Do you like the new schedule better?
Way better. Also, we're migrating from Kafka to Pulsar. The multi-tenancy support is what sold the team. Kafka served us well but Pulsar fits our new architecture better.
Pulsar's built-in multi-tenancy is a big deal if you're going multi-tenant. That's a major infrastructure shift though.
Oh also, I switched from coffee to matcha. I know, I know — very trendy. But my energy levels are way more stable throughout the day. No more 3pm crashes.
Hey, if it works, it works. The L-theanine in matcha does smooth out the caffeine curve compared to coffee. How's the Pulsar migration going?
Almost done actually. We've migrated about 80% of the topics. The 2pm standup has been great for coordinating with the EU team on the migration — everyone's actually awake and engaged.
That's good progress. Having the whole team in sync for a migration like that makes a huge difference.

queries.question: What messaging system are they using?
What do they drink in the morning?
What changed recently at their work?
